matches.x: 2 And the people cried unto Moses; and. when Moses prayed unto the Lord, the fire was quenched.

(Bamidbar 11:2) "And the people cried out to Moses": How would Moses help them? Should it not be "And the people cried out to the L-rd"? — R. Shimon says: An analogy: A king was angry with his son, and he went to the king's loved one and said to him: Please intercede for me with father. Thus, Israel went to him: Please intercede for us with the L-rd. I might think that Moses would demur; it is,…

The hardships of life in the wilderness arouse nostalgia for life in Egypt. Another popular idealizing of the past is chronicled in Numbers 11:5: "We remember the fish that we used to eat free in Egypt, the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic." (Nahum Sarna, The JPS Torah Commentary: Exodus, Philadelphia, 1989, 85-86)

The Joyful Burden of Discipleship By Elder Ronald A. Rasband Of the Presidency of the Seventy To sustain our leaders is a privilege; it comes coupled with a personal responsibility to share their burden and to be disciples of the Lord.
